languageExamples.description: While a direct shift from [m] to [ts] is not typical, intermediate epenthetic stages can be observed. For example, 'warmth' /wɔːrmθ/ can be pronounced with an epenthetic [p] as [wɔːrmpθ], and 'something' as [sʌmpθɪŋ]. This shows a similar process of stop insertion after a nasal, though the end result is not [ts].

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
preamble: A complex transformation from a bilabial nasal [m] to a voiceless alveolar affricate [ts]. This change is highly unusual and would likely proceed through several stages, such as epenthesis ([m] -> [mpt]) followed by further shifts.
